docs.title: Chaos API
docs.text: A FastAPI application that accepts any endpoint and any HTTP method, then uses an LLM (via LangChain + OpenAI) to generate a realistic fake REST API response. The model infers what API would logically exist at the requested path and returns plausible data — not placeholder text.

docs.title: Concept
docs.text: Send a request to /users/42 and get back a realistic JSON user object. Hit /weather/london with Accept: application/xml and receive a well-formed XML weather response. The API pretends to be whatever service would plausibly live at that URL.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Universal Endpoint: Accepts any path and HTTP method
  • Content Negotiation: Supports JSON, XML, HTML, and plain text responses
  • Production Ready: Rate limiting, request logging, health checks, and metrics
  • Dockerized: Ready-to-use Docker images for easy deployment
  • Well Tested: Comprehensive test suite with 80%+ coverage
  • Type Safe: Fully typed with mypy

docs.title: Using Docker
docs.text: docker run -p 8000:8000 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key johnsosoka/chaos-api

docs.title: Using Docker Compose
docs.text:
# Copy and configure environment
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your OpenAI API key

docker-compose up chaos-api

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
# Install dependencies
poetry install

# Configure environment
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your OpenAI API key

# Run the server
poetry run uvicorn src.chaos_api.main:app --reload --port 8000

docs.title: JSON API (default)
docs.text:
# List users
curl http://localhost:8000/users

# Get a specific user
curl http://localhost:8000/users/42

# Create an order
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/orders \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"product_id": "abc123", "quantity": 2}'

docs.title: XML Response
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/weather/london \
  -H "Accept: application/xml"

docs.title: HTML Response
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/products \
  -H "Accept: text/html"

docs.title: Plain Text
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/health \
  -H "Accept: text/plain"

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/health

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/metrics

docs.title: Supported Response Types
docs.text:
MIME Type Description application/json JSON (default) application/xml XML text/html HTML page or fragment text/plain Plain text

The response type is determined by the Accept header. Defaults to application/json when no Accept header is present or no supported type matches.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    Client[Client Request] --> Middleware[Middleware Layer]
    Middleware --> Router[FastAPI Router]
    Router --> Handler[Request Handler]
    Handler --> MIME[MIME Handler]
    Handler --> Prompt[Prompt Builder]
    Prompt --> LLM[LLM Client LangChain + OpenAI]
    LLM --> Handler
    Handler --> Response[Response]

    subgraph Middleware
        RequestID[Request ID]
        RateLimit[Rate Limiting]
        Logging[Request Logging]
    end

    subgraph Routes
        Health[/health]
        Metrics[/metrics]
        CatchAll[/{path}]
    end

docs.title: Component Overview
docs.text:
• Request Handler: Extracts request context (method, path, headers, body)
  • MIME Handler: Parses Accept headers and formats request bodies
  • Prompt Builder: Constructs LLM prompts from request context
  • LLM Client: Wraps LangChain/OpenAI for response generation
  • Middleware: Request ID tracing, rate limiting, and logging

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
All configuration is done via environment variables:

Variable Default Description OPENAI_API_KEY (required) Your OpenAI API key MODEL_NAME gpt-4o-mini OpenAI model to use LOG_LEVEL INFO Logging level (DEBUG, INFO, etc.) RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS 100 Max requests per window per IP RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW 60 Rate limit window in seconds DEBUG false Enable debug mode

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
# Install dependencies
poetry install

# Install pre-commit hooks
poetry run pre-commit install

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
# Run all tests
poetry run pytest tests/ -v

# Run with coverage
poetry run pytest tests/ --cov=src/chaos_api --cov-report=term-missing

docs.title: Code Quality
docs.text:
# Linting
poetry run ruff check src/ tests/
poetry run ruff check --fix src/ tests/

# Formatting
poetry run ruff format src/ tests/

# Type checking
poetry run mypy src/

docs.title: Using the Example Client
docs.text:
# Run all examples
python examples/client.py

# Run specific example
python examples/client.py --example json

# Use with custom URL
python examples/client.py --url http://api.example.com

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
chaos-api/
├── src/chaos_api/          # Main application code
│   ├── main.py             # FastAPI application
│   ├── config.py           # Pydantic settings
│   ├── llm_client.py       # LangChain LLM wrapper
│   ├── mime_handlers.py    # MIME type detection
│   ├── prompt_builder.py   # LLM prompt construction
│   ├── middleware.py       # Request middleware
│   └── routes.py           # Health/metrics endpoints
├── tests/                  # Test suite
├── examples/               # Example client scripts
├── .github/workflows/      # CI/CD configuration
├── Dockerfile              # Production Docker image
├── docker-compose.yml      # Docker Compose configuration
└── pyproject.toml          # Poetry dependencies

docs.title: Catch-All Endpoint
docs.text:
• URL: /{path:path}
  • Methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS, TRACE
  • Description: Generates fake API responses for any path

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
• URL: /health
  • Method: GET
  • Response: {"status": "healthy", "version": "0.1.0", "uptime_seconds": 123}

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
• URL: /metrics
  • Method: GET
  • Response: {"uptime_seconds": 123, "total_requests": 42, ...}

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
# Build image
docker build -t chaos-api .

# Run container
docker run -p 8000:8000 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=your-key chaos-api

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
# Production
docker-compose up -d chaos-api

# Development with hot reload
docker-compose --profile dev up -d chaos-api-dev

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines on how to contribute to this project.

docs.text:
• Built with FastAPI
  • LLM integration via LangChain
  • Powered by OpenAI

docs.title: Contributing to Chaos API
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to Chaos API! This document provides guidelines and instructions for contributing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • Poetry 1.8+
  • Docker (optional, for containerized development)

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository 

    git clone https://github.com/johnsosoka/chaos-api.git
cd chaos-api

  2. Install dependencies 

    poetry install

  3. Set up environment variables 

    cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and add your OpenAI API key

  4. Run the development server 

    poetry run uvicorn src.chaos_api.main:app --reload --port 8000

docs.title: Using Docker
docs.text:
# Production build
docker-compose up chaos-api

# Development with hot reload
docker-compose --profile dev up chaos-api-dev

docs.title: Code Quality
docs.text: We use several tools to maintain code quality:

docs.title: Linting and Formatting
docs.text:
# Run linter
poetry run ruff check src/ tests/

# Fix auto-fixable issues
poetry run ruff check --fix src/ tests/

# Format code
poetry run ruff format src/ tests/

docs.title: Type Checking
docs.text: poetry run mypy src/

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
# Run all tests
poetry run pytest tests/ -v

# Run with coverage
poetry run pytest tests/ --cov=src/chaos_api --cov-report=term-missing

docs.title: Pre-commit Hooks
docs.text:
Install pre-commit hooks to run checks automatically:

poetry run pre-commit install

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
chaos-api/
├── src/chaos_api/          # Main application code
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py             # FastAPI application
│   ├── config.py           # Pydantic settings
│   ├── llm_client.py       # LangChain LLM wrapper
│   ├── mime_handlers.py    # MIME type detection
│   ├── prompt_builder.py   # LLM prompt construction
│   ├── middleware.py       # Request middleware
│   └── routes.py           # Health/metrics endpoints
├── tests/                  # Test suite
├── .github/workflows/      # CI/CD configuration
├── Dockerfile              # Production Docker image
├── Dockerfile.dev          # Development Docker image
├── docker-compose.yml      # Docker Compose configuration
├── pyproject.toml          # Poetry dependencies and tool config
└── README.md               # Project documentation

docs.title: Making Changes
docs.text:
1. Create a branch 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

  2. Make your changes

  3. Follow existing code style
  4. Add tests for new functionality

  5. Update documentation as needed

  6. Run quality checks 

    poetry run ruff check src/ tests/
poetry run mypy src/
poetry run pytest tests/

  7. Commit your changes 

    git add .
git commit -m "Add feature: description"

  8. Push and create a pull request 

    git push origin feature/your-feature-name

docs.title: Testing Guidelines
docs.text:
• Write tests for all new functionality
  • Aim for 80%+ code coverage
  • Use descriptive test names that explain what is being tested
  • Mock external dependencies (LLM calls, etc.)
  • Test both success and error cases

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
The application follows a layered architecture:

graph TD
    Client[Client Request]
    Middleware[Middleware Layer<br/>Request ID, Rate Limit, Logging]
    Router[Router Layer<br/>Catch-all endpoint]
    Handler[Request Handler]
    LLM[LLM Client<br/>LangChain + OpenAI]
    Response[Response]

    Client --> Middleware
    Middleware --> Router
    Router --> Handler
    Handler --> LLM
    LLM --> Handler
    Handler --> Response

docs.title: Key Components
docs.text:
• Middleware: Request ID tracing, rate limiting, logging
  • MIME Handlers: Content negotiation and request body parsing
  • Prompt Builder: Constructs LLM prompts from request context
  • LLM Client: Wraps LangChain for API response generation

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text:
1. Update version in pyproject.toml
  2. Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes
  3. Create a git tag: git tag v0.x.x
  4. Push tag: git push origin v0.x.x
  5. GitHub Actions will build and publish the Docker image

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text:
• Be respectful and constructive
  • Focus on what is best for the community
  • Welcome newcomers and help them learn

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text:
Open an issue for: - Bug reports - Feature requests - Documentation improvements - General questions

Thank you for contributing!
